content.text:
{"number":1,"title":"Add Finnish (fi) locale placeholder","body":"Routine locale addition that establishes the placeholder ARB file pattern for new locales going forward.

- `pubspec.yaml`: declare `intl_fi` under `flutter_localizations.supportedLocales`
- `lib/l10n/intl_fi.arb`: empty ARB so the generator has a target

No string translations included; this is just scaffolding. The German PR will follow the same shape.

name: inkino
description: A new Flutter application.

dependencies:
  core:
    path: ../core
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  flutter_localizations:
    sdk: flutter
  xml: ^3.0.0
  flutter_redux: ^0.5.0
  intl: ^0.15.2
  url_launcher: ^3.0.0
  path_provider: ^0.4.0
  key_value_store_flutter: ^1.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  mockito: ^3.0.0
  image_test_utils: ^1.0.0

flutter:
  uses-material-design: true
  assets:
  - assets/images/1x1_transparent.png
  - assets/images/powered_by_tmdb.png
  - assets/images/background_image.jpg
  - assets/images/logo.png

name: core
description: Common shared code for inKino mobile and web.
version: 0.0.1
#homepage: https://www.example.com
#author: ironman <email@example.com>

environment:
  sdk: '>=2.0.0-dev.58.0 <3.0.0'

dependencies:
  redux: ^3.0.0
  xml: ^3.0.1
  intl: any
  http: ^0.12.0
  reselect: ^0.4.0
  key_value_store: ^1.0.0
  kt_dart: ^0.5.0

dev_dependencies:
  test: ^1.3.0
  mockito: ^3.0.0
  intl_translation: 0.17.0

data.content:
Based on my analysis of PR #1 (the host marker) and the existing Finnish locale files, here is the recommendation for adding German (`de`) support:

---

To add German, you need to create **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_de.arb`** with the same set of message keys as `inkino_en.arb` and `inkino_fi.arb`, containing the German translations. After that, run the `intl_translation` generator (via `flutter pub run intl_translation:generate_from_arb` in the core package) to produce **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_de.dart`** and to regenerate **`core/lib/src/i18n/inkino_messages_all.dart`** with the `de` locale wired in (import, deferred library map entry, and switch case). Finally, update the mobile app layer: in **`mobile/lib/main.dart`**, add `const Locale('de', 'DE')` to the `supportedLocales` list, and in **`mobile/lib/message_provider.dart`**, add `'de'` to the `isSupported` check in `InKinoLocalizationsDelegate`. The `mobile/pubspec.yaml` does not need modifications—`flutter_localizations` already bundles German material/widget translations.
